entities.name: GuestImage
entities.description: An assembled and linked bare-metal binary that boots directly in a VM. Produced by GNU as + GNU ld from arch/<isa>/platform/<vm>/ sources.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Filesystem path to the ELF/flat binary

entities.name: VMInstance
entities.description: A running QEMU or Firecracker process hosting a guest image. Launched in the background, polled for readiness, killed cleanly after tests complete. Never use timeout to kill QEMU -- background + poll + kill.

properties.name: pid
properties.description: Host OS process ID of the VM

properties.name: serial_path
properties.description: Path to serial output file (-serial file:<path>)

entities.name: TestCase
entities.description: A single verification to run against a booted VM guest. Checks are performed from the host side -- either parsing serial output or making HTTP requests to the guest.

properties.name: check_type
properties.description: serial: check serial output file for expected markers. http: make HTTP request and verify response.

entities.name: TestResult
entities.description: Pass/fail outcome of a single test case.

relationships.name: boots
relationships.description: A VM instance boots exactly one guest image

relationships.name: runs_against
relationships.description: Test cases run against a booted VM instance

relationships.name: produces
relationships.description: Each test case produces one result

domain_constraints.name: no-native-execution
domain_constraints.description: Assembly code must NEVER be executed natively on the host. All execution happens inside a VM (QEMU or Firecracker).

domain_constraints.name: clean-vm-kill
domain_constraints.description: Never use timeout to kill QEMU. Launch in background, poll serial output file, then kill cleanly. Use -serial file:<path> not -serial mon:stdio.

domain_constraints.name: host-side-verification
domain_constraints.description: All test verification is from the host side. The guest has no test infrastructure inside it -- it just does what it does, and the host observes.

domain_constraints.name: kvm-required-for-firecracker
domain_constraints.description: Firecracker requires /dev/kvm and only works for the native architecture. Skip gracefully if KVM is not available.

modules.name: vm_launcher
modules.responsibility: Launch QEMU or Firecracker VMs in the background, poll for readiness via serial output file, and kill cleanly. Handles both x86_64 and aarch64 with appropriate QEMU system binary and flags.

classes.name: VMConfig
classes.description: Immutable configuration for launching a VM

functions.name: launch_vm
functions.docstring: Launch a VM in the background. Returns a handle with pid and serial output path. Does not block -- caller must poll for readiness.
postconditions: VM process is running in background
Serial output file exists (may be empty)

functions.name: wait_for_ready
functions.docstring: Poll serial output file until marker string appears or timeout. Returns True if ready, False if timed out.

functions.name: kill_vm
functions.docstring: Kill the VM process cleanly via SIGTERM, then SIGKILL if needed. Wait for process to exit.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test with mock subprocess. Functional test with a minimal guest image that writes a known marker to serial output, then verifies wait_for_ready and kill_vm work correctly. Test the KVM-not-available path by mocking os.access.

modules.name: guest_builder
modules.responsibility: Assemble and link guest images using GNU as cross-toolchains and GNU ld. Selects the correct toolchain based on target arch. Returns the path to the built binary.

functions.name: build_guest
functions.docstring: Assemble and link the guest image for the given arch/platform. Returns path to the output binary.

functions.name: toolchain_for_arch
functions.docstring: Return (assembler, linker) binary names for the target arch. E.g., ('aarch64-linux-gnu-as', 'aarch64-linux-gnu-ld').

modules.test_strategy: Unit test toolchain_for_arch with known arches. Functional test build_guest with a minimal .S file that assembles and links successfully. Test missing-toolchain path by mocking subprocess.run to raise FileNotFoundError.

modules.name: test_runner
modules.responsibility: Orchestrate the full test cycle: build guest, launch VM, wait for ready, run test cases, kill VM, report results. This is the entry point for both pre-commit and CI.

classes.name: TestSuite
classes.description: Collection of test cases for a target

classes.name: SuiteResult
classes.description: Aggregate results for a test suite run

functions.name: run_suite
functions.docstring: Run a complete test suite: build, boot, test, kill, report.

functions.name: check_serial
functions.docstring: Check serial output file for expected content.

functions.name: check_http
functions.docstring: Make HTTP GET request and verify response body matches expected.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test check_serial and check_http with fixtures. Integration test run_suite with a minimal guest that boots, emits a serial marker, and optionally serves HTTP. Test all failure paths: build failure, boot timeout, check mismatch, VM crash.

modules.name: cli
modules.responsibility: Command-line interface for the test harness. Supports running a single arch/platform combo or the full matrix. Reports pass/fail per test with exit code 0 on all-pass, 1 on any failure.

functions.name: main
functions.docstring: Entry point. Parse args, run tests, report, return exit code.

modules.test_strategy: Test argument parsing. Test exit codes for all-pass and any-failure scenarios with mocked test_runner.

external_dependencies.name: pydantic
external_dependencies.reason: Data models for VMConfig, TestSuite, results
